behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration was plausible because route quality uses distance + heuristic logic. Pass 2: the failure boundary in this behavior is the heuristic quality rule itself, which can be exercised in-process with fixed coordinate sets and a local optimal baseline calculator. Rejected higher levels: the behavior text only requires 'heuristic produces a route <=1.5x optimal' and does not require OS/network/subprocess, AI output, or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test the heuristic function with deterministic stop sets (including edge cases and randomized seeds), compute reference optimal (exact for small n, trusted benchmark fixtures for larger n), and assert route_length/optimal_length <= 1.5 for each case up to 20 stops.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: could be Integration if config and segment modules are separate. Pass 2: minimum failure boundary is arithmetic + road-type speed lookup, which is local and deterministic. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely 'computed correctly from distance ... and configured average speed', with no real infra, no model judgment, and no staged agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test the estimator with table-driven cases covering multiple road types, boundary distances, rounding rules, and mixed segments; assert per-segment and total time-window outputs match expected formulas.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration considered if total time is produced by another module. Pass 2: this behavior's own failure mode is validator decision + excess calculation, testable at one object/function boundary with numeric inputs. Rejected higher levels: text specifies only 'rejects ... exceeds ... returning excess amount'; no OS/network/subprocess, no AI dependency, no multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test validator with exact-threshold, just-under, just-over, and large-overage inputs; assert rejection flag and excess value are correct and stable under rounding/units conversions.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: System considered because geocoding is an external API. Pass 2 (integration brake): (a) real OS/network not required to trigger this specific failure mode; an in-process geocoder stub that returns 'no results' reproduces it. (b) no AI output dependency. (c) no multi-agent sequence. This behavior is about module handoff: geocode result -> filter skipped -> optimizer input -> report output. Rejected System due behavior text focusing on 'API returns no results' outcome handling, not real HTTP transport behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring service + geocode client interface (stubbed response map) + optimizer + reporting: provide addresses where one geocode lookup returns empty, assert skipped list contains that address, remaining stops are optimized, and skipped stop is absent from route.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Unit considered for dedup function only. Pass 2: real failure often appears at handoff boundary (dedup + item merge + route construction), where duplicates can reappear or items can be lost. Integration is minimum to catch that interaction. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is local data orchestration ('consolidated ... combined delivery items ... not visited twice'), with no real OS/network/subprocess, no AI model output, no agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test through request-to-route assembly pipeline: input duplicate addresses with distinct item lists, assert one route stop exists for that address, combined items are preserved exactly once, and visit count for that location is one.
